title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Department of the Interior relating to "Oil and Gas and Sulfur Operations in the Outer Continental Shelf-High Pressure High Temperature Updates".
plain_summary: This bill lets Congress reject a rule made by the Department of the Interior about drilling for oil and gas in ocean waters. The rule deals with high pressure and high temperature drilling operations. If Congress passes this bill, the Department of the Interior rule would be canceled and would not be allowed to take effect. This means the new drilling rules would not become law.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress decides to reject a rule that the Department of the Interior created. This rule is called Oil and Gas and Sulfur Operations in the Outer Continental Shelf High Pressure High Temperature Updates. The rule was published in the Federal Register (the government's official daily publication) on August thirtieth, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress is rejecting this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced.
